ucs.title: [LLM] DarkVishnya rogue physical device — unmanaged internal IP performing port-sweep + SMB/RDP brute force
ucs.description: DarkVishnya's signature kill-chain begins with a physically planted Bash Bunny / Raspberry Pi / netbook attached to an in-branch LAN port (T1200). The implant then enumerates the internal estate (T1046, T1135) and brute-forces domain accounts over SMB/RDP (T1110). Detecting the implant means correlating an internal source IP that (a) does not belong to any MDE-managed device and (b) both port-scans and password-sprays many internal hosts in a short window — a combination almost unique to this actor's bank intrusions reported by Securelist (Dec 2018).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count, dc(All_Traffic.dest) as scanned_hosts, dc(All_Traffic.dest_port) as scanned_ports, values(All_Traffic.dest) as host_sample, values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as port_sample, min(_time) as first_seen from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.src_category!="managed_endpoint" All_Traffic.src_ip=10.0.0.0/8 OR All_Traffic.src_ip=172.16.0.0/12 OR All_Traffic.src_ip=192.168.0.0/16 by All_Traffic.src_ip _time span=30m | rename All_Traffic.* as * | where scanned_hosts>=10 AND scanned_ports>=8 | join type=inner src_ip _time [ | tstats summariesonly=true count as failed_logons, dc(Authentication.user) as failed_accounts from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action="failure" Authentication.src=10.0.0.0/8 OR Authentication.src=172.16.0.0/12 OR Authentication.src=192.168.0.0/16 by Authentication.src _time span=30m | rename Authentication.src as src_ip | where failed_logons>=20 OR failed_accounts>=5 ] | sort - first_seen
ucs.kql:
let Window = 30m;
let DstHostThreshold = 10;
let PortThreshold = 8;
let FailedLogonThreshold = 20;
let AccountSprayThreshold = 5;
// Build the set of IPs that belong to managed (MDE-onboarded) devices
let ManagedIPs = DeviceNetworkInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
    | mv-expand IPRow = IPAddresses
    | extend ip = tostring(IPRow.IPAddress)
    | where isnotempty(ip)
    | summarize by ip;
// Inbound connection accept events on managed hosts, sourced from an internal IP that is NOT a managed device
let Scanners = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
    | where ActionType in ("InboundConnectionAccepted", "ConnectionAttempt")
    | where ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP) and not(RemoteIP startswith "127.") and not(RemoteIP startswith "169.254.")
    | where RemoteIP !in (ManagedIPs)
    | summarize ScannedHosts = dcount(DeviceId),
                ScannedPorts = dcount(LocalPort),
                ScanFirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
                Hosts = make_set(DeviceName, 50),
                Ports = make_set(LocalPort, 50)
            by RogueIP = RemoteIP, bin(Timestamp, Window)
    | where ScannedHosts >= DstHostThreshold and ScannedPorts >= PortThreshold;
let BruteForce = IdentityLogonEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
    | where ActionType == "LogonFailed"
    | where ipv4_is_private(IPAddress)
    | summarize FailedLogons = count(),
                FailedAccounts = dcount(AccountName),
                BFFirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
                AccountSample = make_set(AccountName, 25)
            by RogueIP = IPAddress, bin(Timestamp, Window)
    | where FailedLogons >= FailedLogonThreshold or FailedAccounts >= AccountSprayThreshold;
Scanners
| join kind=inner BruteForce on RogueIP, Timestamp
| project ScanFirstSeen, BFFirstSeen, RogueIP, ScannedHosts, ScannedPorts,
          FailedLogons, FailedAccounts, Hosts, Ports, AccountSample
| order by ScanFirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let Window = 30m;
let DstHostThreshold = 10;
let PortThreshold = 8;
let FailedLogonThreshold = 20;
let AccountSprayThreshold = 5;
// Build allowlist of corporate-managed device IPs from heartbeats
let ManagedIPs = Heartbeat
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
    | summarize by ComputerIP
    | union (Heartbeat
        | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
        | mv-expand IP = split(ComputerPrivateIPs, ",")
        | project ComputerIP = trim(" ", tostring(IP)));
let Scanners = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
    | where NetworkDirection == "Inbound"
    | where ipv4_is_private(SrcIpAddr) and not(SrcIpAddr startswith "127.") and not(SrcIpAddr startswith "169.254.")
    | where SrcIpAddr !in (ManagedIPs)
    | summarize ScannedHosts = dcount(DvcHostname),
                ScannedPorts = dcount(DstPortNumber),
                ScanFirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
                Hosts = make_set(DvcHostname, 50),
                Ports = make_set(DstPortNumber, 50)
            by RogueIP = SrcIpAddr, bin(TimeGenerated, Window)
    | where ScannedHosts >= DstHostThreshold and ScannedPorts >= PortThreshold;
let BruteForce = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
    | where EventID == 4625
    | where AccountType == "User"
    | where ipv4_is_private(IpAddress)
    | summarize FailedLogons = count(),
                FailedAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName),
                BFFirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
                AccountSample = make_set(TargetUserName, 25)
            by RogueIP = IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, Window)
    | where FailedLogons >= FailedLogonThreshold or FailedAccounts >= AccountSprayThreshold;
Scanners
| join kind=inner BruteForce on RogueIP, TimeGenerated
| project ScanFirstSeen, BFFirstSeen, RogueIP, ScannedHosts, ScannedPorts,
          FailedLogons, FailedAccounts, Hosts, Ports, AccountSample
| order by ScanFirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Most APTs reach the internal LAN via phishing or VPN credentials and operate from a managed endpoint. DarkVishnya uniquely starts from an attacker-owned device physically jacked into the branch LAN, so its source IP belongs to no MDE/AD-joined host. The dual signal — same unmanaged internal IP scanning >=10 hosts on >=8 ports AND brute-forcing >=5 accounts in a 30-minute window — is the documented

ucs.title: [LLM] DarkVishnya backdoor RAT installed as Windows service beaconing on a non-standard port
ucs.description: After the rogue device pivots, DarkVishnya plants commodity remote-access tooling (DameWare, RMS/Remote Manipulator System, Radmin, Impacket-launched binaries) registered as a Windows service (T1543.003) and configured to beacon to an external relay over a non-standard port (T1571) — Securelist specifically called out the actor's habit of moving C2 onto rare ports to evade firewall ACLs that allow only 80/443/53. This UC chains services.exe spawning a remote-management binary with that same PID immediately egressing on a port outside the common allow-list.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count, min(_time) as proc_time, values(Processes.process) as proc_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="services.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("rms.exe","r_server.exe","rutserv.exe","radmin.exe","radmin3.exe","anydesk.exe","anydeskmsi.exe","teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","lmiguardiansvc.exe","dwrcs.exe","dwrcst.exe","tvnserver.exe","screenconnect.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","atera.exe","ateraagent.exe","supremo.exe","aa_v3.exe","strwinclt.exe","meshagent.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_id _time | rename Processes.* as * | join type=inner dest process_id [ | tstats summariesonly=true count as conn_count, values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as remote_ip, values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as remote_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.action="allowed" NOT (All_Traffic.dest_ip=10.0.0.0/8 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=172.16.0.0/12 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=192.168.0.0/16) NOT (All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,53,5938,7070)) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_id | rename All_Traffic.src as dest All_Traffic.process_id as process_id ] | table proc_time, dest, user, process_name, proc_cmd, remote_ip, remote_port, conn_count | sort - proc_time
ucs.kql:
let RemoteAccessBins = dynamic([
    "rms.exe","r_server.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe",
    "radmin.exe","radmin3.exe","raddrv.dll",
    "anydesk.exe","anydeskmsi.exe",
    "teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","tv_w32.exe",
    "lmiguardiansvc.exe","lmi_rescue.exe",
    "dwrcs.exe","dwrcst.exe",
    "tvnserver.exe","winvnc.exe",
    "screenconnect.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe",
    "atera.exe","ateraagent.exe",
    "supremo.exe","aa_v3.exe","strwinclt.exe","meshagent.exe"
]);
// Standard ports these tools normally use plus generic web — anything else is suspicious egress
let StandardC2Ports = dynamic([80, 443, 53, 5938, 7070, 6568, 8200]);
let RatServices = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "services.exe"
    | where FileName in~ (RemoteAccessBins)
    | project ProcStartTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              SvcFileName = FileName, SvcCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              SvcPid = ProcessId, SvcSha256 = SHA256;
RatServices
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemotePort !in (StandardC2Ports)
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, NetPid = InitiatingProcessId,
              RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl,
              NetProcFile = InitiatingProcessFileName
  ) on DeviceId
| where NetPid == SvcPid
| where NetTime between (ProcStartTime .. ProcStartTime + 6h)
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstBeacon = min(NetTime),
            LastBeacon = max(NetTime),
            RemoteIPs = make_set(RemoteIP, 25),
            RemotePorts = make_set(RemotePort, 25),
            RemoteUrls = make_set(RemoteUrl, 25)
        by DeviceName, AccountName, SvcFileName, SvcCmd, SvcSha256, ProcStartTime
| order by ProcStartTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let RemoteAccessBins = dynamic([
    "rms.exe","r_server.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe",
    "radmin.exe","radmin3.exe",
    "anydesk.exe","anydeskmsi.exe",
    "teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","tv_w32.exe",
    "lmiguardiansvc.exe","lmi_rescue.exe",
    "dwrcs.exe","dwrcst.exe",
    "tvnserver.exe","winvnc.exe",
    "screenconnect.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe",
    "atera.exe","ateraagent.exe",
    "supremo.exe","aa_v3.exe","strwinclt.exe","meshagent.exe"
]);
let StandardC2Ports = dynamic([80, 443, 53, 5938, 7070, 6568, 8200]);
// Service install events (4697 in Security log; 7045 also possible in System)
let RatServices = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4697
    | extend SvcFile = tolower(tostring(extract(@"([^\\\/]+\.exe)", 1, ServiceFileName)))
    | where SvcFile in~ (RemoteAccessBins)
    | project ProcStartTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account = SubjectAccount,
              SvcName = ServiceName, SvcFile, ServiceFileName, SvcStartType = ServiceStartType;
RatServices
| join kind=inner (
    ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventResult == "Success"
    | where NetworkDirection == "Outbound"
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr))
    | where DstPortNumber !in (StandardC2Ports)
    | extend ProcFile = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ProcFile in~ (RemoteAccessBins)
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ProcFile,
              DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url
  ) on $left.Computer == $right.DvcHostname, $left.SvcFile == $right.ProcFile
| where NetTime between (ProcStartTime .. ProcStartTime + 6h)
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstBeacon = min(NetTime),
            LastBeacon = max(NetTime),
            RemoteIPs = make_set(DstIpAddr, 25),
            RemotePorts = make_set(DstPortNumber, 25),
            RemoteUrls = make_set(Url, 25)
        by Computer, Account, SvcName, SvcFile, ServiceFileName, ProcStartTime
| order by ProcStartTime desc
ucs.rationale: Banks legitimately use one or two managed RMM tools, but DarkVishnya's reported pattern is to drop a SECOND, unsanctioned remote-access binary as a service after lateral movement and intentionally relocate its callback off 443/5938 to bypass branch firewall egress ACLs (T1571). The chain — services.exe → unsanctioned RMM binary → same PID egressing to a public IP on a non-standard port within the

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, SecurityEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
